title: Observed Pit-Wave Context Features
summary: F1 strategy calls often cluster: teams cover rivals, react to safety-car windows, or join a pit window.
rationale: F1 strategy calls often cluster: teams cover rivals, react to safety-car windows, or join a pit window. The dataset includes current-lap PitStop, so same-race/lap field activity can proxy strategy-wave pressure without using PitNextLap.
implementation_hint: For each Year-Race-LapNumber, compute count/rate of PitStop among other cars, plus rolling previous 1-3 lap pit counts by race, by compound, and by position band. Exclude the row itself from same-lap counts to avoid self-signal leakage.
expected_effect: Adds orthogonal race-state signal beyond individual tyre wear; should improve ranking near mass pit windows.
risk: If train/test rows are not complete race fields, same-lap counts may be noisy; ensure features are computed separately inside each fold or from non-target columns only.
